title:
EEG: Electrophysiological biomarkers of behavioral dimensions 
from cross-species paradigms
description: This dataset comprises electrophysiological recordings from 57 human subjects performing three different behavioral tasks designed to identify cross-species electrophysiological biomarkers of behavioral dimensions. The study employs EEG to characterize neural correlates of behavior using paradigms translatable across species, with data collected in San Diego (2014-2016) and analyzed in New Mexico (2015-2021). The dataset includes trigger information and Matlab processing scripts for signal translation and analysis.
methods_description: EEG data were collected from 57 human subjects performing three different tasks. Triggers were encoded as odd binary recombinations and re-translated into 0-255 values using Matlab scripts. Data collection occurred circa 2014-2016.
